response.justification: Szabo demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and has proven experience with large-scale infrastructure systems, microservices, and cloud architecture. However, there is a significant gap in the minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ programming—his last C++ work was ~10 years ago with only 6 months of recorded depth, while the past decade focuses on TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang. His technical leadership is established but not specifically in cloud or DevOps engineering. While his rapid language adaptability and infrastructure experience could bridge the gap, the C++ requirement and systems specialization shortfall create material risk.
strengths: Extensive experience shipping products at scale, including high-traffic infrastructure at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec) demonstrating large-scale system design capability
Clear track record of technical leadership and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple Staff-level roles
Proven cloud architecture and infrastructure experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, microservices, GISCollective distributed systems design)
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap: only ~6 months of recorded C++ work (2014-2015) against the 8-year minimum requirement; last decade is JavaScript/TypeScript-focused
No demonstrated technical leadership specifically within cloud or DevOps engineering; Staff roles appear primarily product and platform-focused rather than infrastructure-specialized
Career pattern shows breadth across many technologies rather than the deep systems and compute infrastructure expertise typically required for this mid-senior role
